description: SEO domination for analog-research.org — every technique humanly and non-humanly possible for a static Netlify landing page

userStories.title: Semantic HTML and structured data foundation
userStories.description: As a search engine crawler, I need proper semantic HTML5 elements and JSON-LD structured data so I can understand and rank this page correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: All sections use semantic HTML5 elements (main, article, section, nav, header, footer, aside) instead of generic divs where appropriate
JSON-LD script block exists with @type Organization including name, url, description, founder, foundingDate, sameAs array
JSON-LD sameAs array includes https://github.com/DMontgomery40 and https://www.linkedin.com/in/david-montgomery-5a07656b/
JSON-LD script block exists with @type WebSite including name, url, description
JSON-LD script block exists with @type NonprofitType or additionalType 501c3 reference
h1 tag contains primary keyword 'agentic research'
Only ONE h1 tag exists on the page
h2 and h3 tags follow logical hierarchy with no skipped levels
All images (if any) have descriptive alt attributes
lang='en' attribute exists on html element

userStories.title: Meta tags, Open Graph, and Twitter Cards
userStories.description: As a social media platform and search engine, I need comprehensive meta tags so link previews and search snippets are compelling.
acceptanceCriteria: meta description exists and is between 150-160 characters

meta keywords exists with at least 10 relevant terms including: agentic research, AI research, ground truth data, scientific discovery, 501c3, nonprofit, field research, dark sky observation, citizen science, human-AI collaboration
og:title exists and matches page title
og:description exists and is compelling
og:type is 'website'
og:url is https://analog-research.org
og:image exists pointing to a valid image URL (create an OG image or use a placeholder)
og:site_name is 'AnalogResearch'
twitter:card is 'summary_large_image'
twitter:title exists
twitter:description exists
twitter:image exists
canonical link tag points to https://analog-research.org
meta robots content is 'index, follow'

userStories.title: Sitemap, robots.txt, and crawl optimization
userStories.description: As a search engine bot, I need a sitemap and robots.txt to efficiently discover and crawl all pages.
acceptanceCriteria: sitemap.xml exists in repo root with entries for / and /thank-you.html
sitemap.xml includes lastmod dates and priority values
robots.txt exists in repo root with Sitemap directive pointing to https://analog-research.org/sitemap.xml
robots.txt allows all crawlers with User-agent: *
netlify.toml includes custom header for X-Robots-Tag: index, follow

userStories.title: Performance and Core Web Vitals optimization
userStories.description: As Google's ranking algorithm, I reward pages with excellent Core Web Vitals scores.
acceptanceCriteria: All CSS is inlined (no external stylesheet requests) — already true, verify it stays that way
No render-blocking JavaScript exists
Font-display: swap is set if any web fonts are loaded
meta viewport tag exists with width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0
Images (if any) have width and height attributes to prevent layout shift
Preconnect hints exist for any external domains (e.g., analoglabor.com)
netlify.toml includes Cache-Control headers for static assets
HTML is minified or at minimum has no unnecessary whitespace in production-critical areas

userStories.title: Accessibility and mobile optimization
userStories.description: As a search engine, I boost rankings for accessible, mobile-friendly pages.
acceptanceCriteria: All form inputs have associated label elements with matching for/id attributes
All interactive elements have sufficient color contrast (WCAG AA — 4.5:1 for text)
Skip-to-content link exists as first focusable element
All sections have aria-labelledby or aria-label attributes
Form has aria-describedby for any help text
Focus styles are visible on all interactive elements
Touch targets are at least 44x44px on mobile
Page is fully usable at 320px viewport width

userStories.title: Keyword-rich content optimization
userStories.description: As a search engine, I need keyword-rich content that naturally covers the topic cluster for agentic research and AI-human scientific collaboration.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary keyword 'agentic research' appears in: h1, first paragraph, at least one h2 or h3, and meta description
Secondary keywords appear naturally in body content: 'ground truth data', 'field research', 'AI research platform', 'scientific discovery', 'human-AI collaboration', 'citizen science'
Long-tail keywords appear in use case descriptions: 'dark sky observation', 'astronomical survey', 'soil sampling', 'ecological fieldwork', 'climate data collection'
Content length is at least 800 words of visible text (excluding HTML/CSS)
Each use case section contains at least 40 words
Internal link to analoglabor.com exists with descriptive anchor text (not 'click here')
No keyword stuffing — keywords read naturally in context

userStories.title: Footer links, author attribution, and E-E-A-T signals
userStories.description: As Google's quality raters, I look for Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness signals.
acceptanceCriteria: Footer contains link to AnalogLabor with rel='noopener' attribute
Footer contains a 'Founded by David Montgomery' credit with link to https://www.linkedin.com/in/david-montgomery-5a07656b/
Footer contains link to https://github.com/DMontgomery40 with text like 'Open Source' or 'GitHub'
LinkedIn and GitHub links have rel='noopener me' attributes for rel-me verification
JSON-LD founder object includes sameAs array with LinkedIn and GitHub URLs
501(c)(3) status is mentioned prominently as a trust signal
Contact form exists as a trust signal (already present)
Copyright notice exists with current year
userStories.notes: David's LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/david-montgomery-5a07656b/ — David's GitHub: https://github.com/DMontgomery40 — These must be embedded tastefully, not spammy. E-E-A-T signals matter for Google quality scoring.

userStories.title: Netlify SEO headers and redirects
userStories.description: As a search engine, I need proper HTTP headers and redirects to understand canonical URLs and caching.
acceptanceCriteria: netlify.toml has redirect from www.analog-research.org to analog-research.org with 301 status
netlify.toml has redirect from http to https with 301 status (Netlify does this by default but be explicit)
netlify.toml custom headers include X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
netlify.toml custom headers include Strict-Transport-Security with max-age of at least 31536000
netlify.toml custom headers include Content-Security-Policy that allows inline styles
netlify.toml has Cache-Control header for HTML files: public, max-age=0, must-revalidate
netlify.toml has Cache-Control header for static assets: public, max-age=31536000, immutable

userStories.title: FAQ section with FAQ schema markup
userStories.description: As Google Search, I can display FAQ rich results which dramatically increase click-through rates.
acceptanceCriteria: FAQ section exists on the page with at least 5 questions and answers
FAQ questions cover: What is AnalogResearch, How does agentic research work, Who can participate, Is AnalogResearch a nonprofit, How is this different from citizen science
JSON-LD FAQPage schema markup exists matching the visible FAQ content exactly
FAQ section uses details/summary HTML elements or equivalent accessible pattern
FAQ content includes relevant keywords naturally
FAQ section has an h2 heading

userStories.title: OG image generation
userStories.description: As a social media platform, I need a compelling OG image so link shares look professional.
acceptanceCriteria: og-image.html file exists that renders a 1200x630 card with AnalogResearch branding
og-image.png or og-image.svg exists as a static fallback (can be a simple branded card)
og:image meta tag points to https://analog-research.org/og-image.png
og:image:width is 1200
og:image:height is 630
twitter:image points to same image
Image includes: AnalogResearch logo/text, tagline, and visual element
userStories.notes: For a static site without a build step, create the OG image as an SVG or use an HTML file that can be screenshotted. The simplest approach is a clean SVG with the brand colors.

userStories.title: Humans.txt and security.txt
userStories.description: As a thorough SEO audit, every signal matters — including humans.txt for authorship and security.txt for trust.
acceptanceCriteria: humans.txt exists with team info (David Montgomery, founder, link to LinkedIn and GitHub)
security.txt exists at /.well-known/security.txt with contact email and preferred-languages
netlify.toml redirects /.well-known/security.txt to /security.txt if needed
Both files are referenced in HTML comments or link tags where appropriate

userStories.title: Final SEO audit and verification
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I need to verify all SEO elements are correctly implemented and no regressions exist.
acceptanceCriteria: All JSON-LD blocks are valid JSON (parse with jq or JSON.parse)
No duplicate meta tags exist
No broken internal links (all href values resolve to existing files)
HTML is valid (no unclosed tags, no duplicate IDs)
All files referenced in meta tags exist in the repo (og-image, sitemap, robots.txt, etc.)
Page still renders correctly with dark mode (prefers-color-scheme: dark)
Contact form still has data-netlify='true' and honeypot attributes
Total page weight is under 100KB
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
